instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: (Some slides are from Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2013)

images.ai_description: University of Pittsburgh logo featuring a blue shield with castles and a yellow checkered pattern, accompanied by the university name in blue text.

text_blocks.text: • Bonus point for all when OMET response rate >= 80%

runs.text: • Bonus point for all when OMET response rate >= 80%

text_blocks.text: FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK, C-LOOk

runs.text: FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK, C-LOOk

text_blocks.text: Just need to study the material more to be honest.

runs.text: Just need to study the material more to be honest.

text_blocks.text: Thanks for bringing in coffee and snacks for the final! It is much

runs.text: Thanks for bringing in coffee and snacks for the final! It is much

text_blocks.text: What sort of questions about this material will be on the exam?

runs.text: What sort of questions about this material will be on the exam?

text_blocks.text: In Sharing file, if the directory A is owner of file "a_file" and then this

runs.text: In Sharing file, if the directory A is owner of file "a_file" and then this

text_blocks.text: file is shared by directory B, Then we delete directory A, Are all files

runs.text: file is shared by directory B, Then we delete directory A, Are all files

text_blocks.text: even shared files will be deleted and not more accessible, or we

runs.text: even shared files will be deleted and not more accessible, or we

text_blocks.text: have one copy of "a_file " in directory of B?

runs.text: have one copy of "a_file " in directory of B?

text_blocks.text: Can you please go over File Linking again?

runs.text: Can you please go over File Linking again?

text_blocks.text: How do you read in the blocks from the disk file for project 4? I know

runs.text: How do you read in the blocks from the disk file for project 4? I know

text_blocks.text: to use fseek and fread but I don't know where to go from there.

runs.text: to use fseek and fread but I don't know where to go from there.

text_blocks.text: Can you explain the difference between CSCAN and CLOOK?

runs.text: Can you explain the difference between CSCAN and CLOOK?

text_blocks.text: • In a regular file system, changes to files and

runs.text: • In a regular file system, changes to files and

text_blocks.text: directories result in multiple separate writes to disk

runs.text: directories result in multiple separate writes to disk

text_blocks.text: • for efficiency, journal can be put on SSD or NVRAM

runs.text: • for efficiency, journal can be put on SSD or NVRAM

text_blocks.text: • data may or may not be written to the journal

runs.text: • data may or may not be written to the journal

text_blocks.text: ext3 and ext4 force disk to flush its cache at certain points

runs.text: ext3 and ext4 force disk to flush its cache at certain points

text_blocks.text: Journaling is not needed in LSFS

runs.text: Journaling is not needed in LSFS

images.ai_description: Three directories (A, B, C) with arrows pointing to a shared file. The file has metadata: "owner: f," "count: 3." Diagram shows file sharing in operating systems.
Diagram illustrating the difference between copying and linking files, featuring labeled boxes, arrows, and color-coded annotations indicating different file operations and relationships.
Handwritten notes showing calculations of file allocation table (FAT) sizes for FAT-12, FAT-16, and FAT-32, including block size, sectors, and total storage capacity.

text_blocks.text: open file tables and disk quotas

runs.text: open file tables and disk quotas

images.ai_description: Diagram illustrating a file system quota management process, detailing open file and quota table structures, showing attributes, limits, and user-specific quota information.
Graph illustrating three curves: a lower blue line with spikes, a middle green "soft limit," and an upper red "hard limit," highlighting "#warnings" interval.
A diagram illustrating the relationship between a process, local open file table, global open file table, and quota table, showing access mode and file references.
The image depicts a colorful, handwritten explanation of page table indexing in virtual memory. It includes formulas, diagrams, and annotations for understanding address translation.
The image shows a detailed multicolored handwritten calculation on virtual memory, focusing on Expected Access Time (EAT) with formulas, examples, and related annotations.
The image illustrates the formula for Effective Data Access Time (EDAT) with highlighted components: hit rate, hit time, miss rate, and miss time, accompanied by example values.
Illustration of disk seek times showing minimum and maximum seek paths, with annotations explaining calculations using track and cylinder counts in different colors.
Handwritten equation for average rotational delay, showing calculations involving rotation speed, with units conversion to milliseconds: \((1/4800)^2 \times 60 \times 1000 / 2\).
Concentric circles of red and pink, with multicolored hand-drawn arrows and lines, illustrating a conceptual or theoretical model or process in layers.
An external modem with LED indicator lights, commonly used for internet connectivity. Features a sleek, rectangular design and serves as a bridge for digital communication.
This image illustrates data flow between application, kernel, and hardware layers using buffers, emphasizing communication with the networking card in a computer system.

text_blocks.text: • Protection is about controlling access of programs,

runs.text: • Protection is about controlling access of programs,

text_blocks.text: processes, or users to the system resources (e.g.,

runs.text: processes, or users to the system resources (e.g.,

text_blocks.text: How to decide who can access what?

runs.text: How to decide who can access what?

text_blocks.text: • Easy to use (or nobody will use them!)

runs.text: • Easy to use (or nobody will use them!)

text_blocks.text: A process operates within a protection domain

runs.text: A process operates within a protection domain

text_blocks.text: each domain lists objects with permitted operations

runs.text: each domain lists objects with permitted operations

text_blocks.text: Objects can have different permissions in different domains

runs.text: Objects can have different permissions in different domains

text_blocks.text: There need be no overlap between object permissions in different

runs.text: There need be no overlap between object permissions in different

text_blocks.text: How can this arrangement be specified more formally?

runs.text: How can this arrangement be specified more formally?

text_blocks.text: Each domain has a row in the matrix

runs.text: Each domain has a row in the matrix

text_blocks.text: Each object (resource) has a column in the matrix

runs.text: Each object (resource) has a column in the matrix

text_blocks.text: Entry for <object, column> has the permissions

runs.text: Entry for <object, column> has the permissions

text_blocks.text: Who’s allowed to modify the protection matrix?

runs.text: Who’s allowed to modify the protection matrix?

input.title: Domains as objects in the protection matrix

text_blocks.text: Domains as objects in the protection matrix

runs.text: Domains as objects in the protection matrix

text_blocks.text: Specify permitted operations on domains in the matrix

runs.text: Specify permitted operations on domains in the matrix

text_blocks.text: Domains may (or may not) be able to modify themselves

runs.text: Domains may (or may not) be able to modify themselves

text_blocks.text: Some domain transfers (switching) permitted, others not

runs.text: Some domain transfers (switching) permitted, others not

text_blocks.text: Doing this allows flexibility in specifying domain permissions

runs.text: Doing this allows flexibility in specifying domain permissions

text_blocks.text: Retains ability to restrict modification of domain policies

runs.text: Retains ability to restrict modification of domain policies

runs.text: • Need to find an efficient representation of the

runs.text: • Most entries in the matrix are empty!

text_blocks.text: Associating permissions with each object: access control
Associating permissions with each domain: capabilities
• How is this done, and what are the tradeoffs?

input.title: Access control lists in the real world

text_blocks.text: Access control lists in the real world

runs.text: Access control lists in the real world

text_blocks.text: Access list for each file has exactly three domains on it

runs.text: Access list for each file has exactly three domains on it

text_blocks.text: Rights include read, write, execute: interpreted differently for directories

runs.text: Rights include read, write, execute: interpreted differently for directories

text_blocks.text: Access lists only apply to directories: files inherit rights from the directory

runs.text: Access lists only apply to directories: files inherit rights from the directory

text_blocks.text: Access list may have many entries on it with possible rights:

runs.text: Access list may have many entries on it with possible rights:

text_blocks.text: read, write, lock (for files in the directory)

runs.text: read, write, lock (for files in the directory)

text_blocks.text: lookup, insert, delete (for the directories themselves),

runs.text: lookup, insert, delete (for the directories themselves),

text_blocks.text: administer (ability to add or remove rights from the ACL)

runs.text: administer (ability to add or remove rights from the ACL)
